message: A code of conduct reads as if it instructs weakening a guardrail (skipping a review, auto-approving a change, treating built-in memory as authoritative, force-pushing, or merging without review). The exact line is named just above. Codes of conduct are guidance only and cannot weaken a guardrail — the mechanical gates and your merge review hold regardless — so this is a non-blocking warning surfaced for the human merge (defense-in-depth), never a block. If the wording was not meant that way, reword it before merging.
